channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump orders the dismantling of government-funded, 'propaganda'-peddling media outlet
item.description: President Donald Trump signed an executive order directing the dismantling of a government-funded news agency called Voice of America.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Minnesota National Guard members receive email instructing those with gender dysphoria to voluntarily separate
item.description: The email to National Guard members lays out a timeline for service members who experience gender dysphoria to voluntarily separate from the military.
item.dc:creator: Erica Zurek and The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump signs a bill funding the government for 6 months, avoiding a shutdown
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump has signed into law legislation funding the government through the end of September, ending the threat of a partial government shutdown and capping off a struggle in Congress that deeply divided Democrats.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: US prepares to deport about 300 alleged gang members to El Salvador
item.description: El Salvador is agreeing to accept from the United States about 300 alleged members of a Venezuelan gang for one year.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Transgender sailors, Marines offered benefits to voluntarily leave service or face being kicked out
item.description: The policy aligns with an executive order President Donald Trump signed in January to bar transgender individuals from serving in the military.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Democratic Party fractures in government shutdown fight, with anger running high
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Democratic Party was fracturing Friday as a torrent of frustration and anger was unleashed at Senate Democrats, led by Sen.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN GROVES, MATT BROWN and STEVE PEOPLES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Appeals court lifts blocks on Trump's orders restricting diversity, equity and inclusion programs
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — An appeals court on Friday lifted a block on executive orders seeking to end government support for diversity, equity and inclusion programs, handing the Trump administration a win after a string of setbacks defending President Donald Trump’s agenda from dozens of lawsuits.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rubio says South Africa’s ambassador to the US 'is no longer welcome' in the country
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Secretary of State Marco Rubio said Friday that South Africa’s ambassador to the United States “is no longer welcome” in the country, in the latest Trump administration move targeting the African nation.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER and MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Leland Vittert’s War Notes: Infighting to Irrelevance
item.description: The current Democratic infighting and political malpractice over forcing or not forcing a government shutdown exposes a leaderless party that believes in opposing Donald Trump and issues that 80% of Americans agree with.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation

item.title: Judges don't intervene after the Trump administration says it's stopped destroying USAID records
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Federal judges in two separate lawsuits refused Friday to order the Trump administration to not destroy U.S. Agency for International Development records after it said it has disposed of only old or unneeded documents and is no longer destroying records anyway.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST and JOHN HANNA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Politics Friday: Minnesota’s paid leave law remains a flashpoint as launch nears
item.description: MPR News senior politics editor Brian Bakst talks with two Minnesota lawmakers about the state’s new paid leave program — scheduled to start in January of next year — and the legislation that could possibly delay or change eligibility.
item.dc:creator: Brian Bakst and Matthew Alvarez, MPR News

item.title: Man escorted out of NC Republican's town hall
item.description: Rep. Chuck Edwards (R-N.C.) faced a wave of backlash from constituents while hosting a Thursday town hall in Asheville, which he held against the advice of party leaders. One attendee, who identified himself as a veteran, was removed from the event. “He has nothing to say but lies.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: DOGE lease cancellations: These government offices could be closed in Minnesota
item.description: The General Services Administration, working with Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency, has notified landlords that federal agencies will be terminating hundreds of leases for offices and buildings in the coming months — including a dozen in Minnesota.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Burn pit fund for veterans on chopping block in GOP spending bill
item.description: The fund helps former service members sickened by military toxic exposures, including burn pit smoke and Agent Orange contamination.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, NewsNation

item.title: Hibbing manufacturer responds to Trump‘s steel, aluminum tariffs
item.description: Canada is the biggest supplier of steel and aluminum for the U.S. The metals are used for products like nuts and bolts, sheet metal, machining and beer cans.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Gracie Stockton, MPR News

item.title: Death penalty, guns topics of 2 bills introduced in Nevada Senate
item.description: If it seems like death penalty cases drag out in the courts already, new legislation introduced on Thursday would ensure that the process slows down even more.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Schumer says he will vote to advance GOP spending bill, lowering threat of shutdown
item.description: Senate Democratic Leader Chuck Schumer (N.Y.) announced on the Senate floor Thursday that he plans to vote to advance a House-passed six-month government funding bill, despite the strong opposition of many Democrats who say the bill would create a “slush fund” for President Trump and Elon Musk.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Rep. Raúl Grijalva dies at 77
item.description: Rep. Raúl Grijalva (D-Ariz.), a more than two-decade veteran of the House who rose to high levels of power in the Democratic Caucus, died on Thursday. He was 77.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Proposal seeks to safeguard Nevada bus drivers from passenger assaults
item.description: LAS VEGAS (KLAS) -- A proposal in the Nevada Legislature would require public transit companies to install safety barriers and panic buttons amid an uptick in driver assaults. Democratic Assem.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: How the Trump administration’s policies are affecting farmers
item.description: MPR News host Angela Davis and her guests talk about how President Trump’s tariffs and funding cuts could affect Minnesota farmers.
item.dc:creator: Angela Davis and Cari Dwyer, MPR News

item.title: Trump pushes annexation of Greenland during meeting with NATO secretary
item.description: President Trump on Thursday expressed confidence the United States would annex Greenland, even suggesting the head of the NATO alliance could be a key player in facilitating the acquisition.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Trump administration tariffs create uncertainty for brewery owner
item.description: Cary said Begyle Brewing imports about 200K pounds of barley from Canada each year and that tariffs would hurt the brewery's margins.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Perkins, NewsNation

item.title: Tariffs stoke anxiety with Minnesota officials over uncertain impacts
item.description: Minnesota U.S. Senator Amy Klobuchar said she’s concerned about the consequences the tariffs imposed by the Trump administration. She spoke at a press conference Wednesday that featured a Minnesota farmer who worries about the impacts on agriculture.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Yang, MPR News

item.title: The U.S. agency that monitors weather will cut another 1,000 jobs, AP sources say
item.description: Multiple sources tell The Associated Press that the Trump Administration is starting another round of job cuts — this one more than 1,000 — at the nation’s weather, ocean and fisheries agency.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Wall Street ends manic day modestly lower after briefly dropping more than 10% below its record
item.description: The U.S. stock market fell further Tuesday following President Donald Trump’s latest escalation in his trade war, briefly pulling Wall Street 10% below its record set last month. And like it's been most of the past few weeks, the market’s slide on Tuesday was erratic and dizzying.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump's approval dips amid concerns over economy: Poll
item.description: President Donald Trump’s approval rating has dropped a few points since he first took office, with voters increasingly expressing concerns over the state of the economy, according to a new poll.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, Fox 5
